input:
Both are very good.” Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, the Prophet’s uncle, who was in his early fifties, said: “By Him who has sent down His Book to you, I shall taste nothing today until I have fought them with my sword outside Madinah.” Al-Nuʿmān ibn Mālik said: “Messenger of God, why do you keep us from heaven? By God Who is the only deity, I will get into it.” The Prophet (peace be upon him) asked him: “On what grounds?” He answered: “Because I am a man who loves God and the Prophet and does not flee from battle.” Iyās ibn Aws said: “It worries me that the Quraysh should be able to return and say: ‘We have besieged Muhammad and his companions in the fortifications and hills of Yathrib [Madinah].’ They will then feel that they can have a go at us at any time.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 20: A Bitter Defeat

output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 20: A Bitter Defeat

characters.name: Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib
characters.appearance: Early 50s, strong athletic build, rugged features. Dark olive skin, thick beard with a bit of grey, well-groomed. Wearing a dark brown bisht with a white turban, brown leather belt. Sandaled feet. Confident and determined expression

characters.name: Al-Nuʿmān ibn Mālik
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, medium build, light skin tone. Thin beard with a reddish tint, neatly kept. Wearing a light brown thobe, white turban, brown leather belt. Sandaled feet. Sincere and determined expression

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, dignified posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak

characters.name: Iyās ibn Aws
characters.appearance: Middle-aged, slender build, dark skin tone. Thin beard with a bit of grey, neatly kept. Wearing a dark brown thobe, brown leather cloak, white turban. Sandaled feet. Concerned expression

output.moral_lesson: Courage and faith can overcome even the greatest challenges.
output.narrative_text: Ḥamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib, the Prophet's uncle, was so angry that he vowed not to eat until he had fought the Quraysh outside Madinah. Al-Nuʿmān ibn Mālik, a companion, told the Prophet that he would enter heaven because he loved God and the Prophet and did not flee from battle.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, companions shown with full faces. Brave and determined scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Late afternoon in a 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A strong rugged older Arab man with dark olive skin, thick beard, dark brown bisht, and white turban, standing confidently with a determined expression. Nearby, a middle-aged Arab man with light skin, thin beard, light brown thobe, and white turban, speaking to another man. In the background, a man with a green cloak and white turban, standing from behind. Woven mat, clay water jug, and a few scattered items. Medium shot, eye level. Warm golden palette. No modern objects.
